title: BUG: select_dtypes ExtensionDtype handling (GH#40234, GH#59888)
description:
- closes #40234
- closes #59888

## Summary

`select_dtypes` routed every spec through `infer_dtype_from_object`, collapsing EA and numpy dtypes into a shared `dtype.type` / `issubclass` matching space. This caused several unrelated issues:

- `select_dtypes("Int64")` and `select_dtypes(pd.Int64Dtype)` silently matched numpy `int64` columns as well as the Int64 EA (GH-40234).
- `select_dtypes(include=["datetime64[ns]"])` silently matched Arrow `timestamp[ns][pyarrow]` columns, and `include=["timestamp[ns][pyarrow]"]` returned empty (GH-59888).
- Passing a parameterized EA class like `pd.ArrowDtype` or `pd.DatetimeTZDtype` as a spec emitted a spurious `UserWarning: Instantiating X without any arguments`.

Resolves the conflation by routing user-facing dtype specs into three distinct buckets:

- **Numpy scalar types** (`np.int64`, `np.dtype("int64")`) — match by `issubclass` on `dtype.type`. Storage-specific.
- **`ExtensionDtype` requests** — class → match any instance of that class (kind), instance → exact `==` (identity).
- **Kind-name strings** (`"int64"`, `"float64"`, `"datetime"`, `"bool"`, ...) — match by `(dtype.kind, dtype.itemsize)` across numpy, masked, and Arrow backends.

The third bucket gives us the kind reading reviewers asked for: `select_dtypes("int64")` selects every int64 column regardless of storage (numpy, Int64Dtype, ArrowDtype), `select_dtypes("datetime")` selects naive numpy, tz-aware DatetimeTZDtype, and Arrow timestamp columns alike. Type-based specs (`np.int64`, `pd.Int64Dtype()`) remain storage-/identity-specific.

EA-parseable strings unify under one rule: bracket-free strings (`"category"`, `"string"`, `"Int64"`) are class (kind) requests, bracketed strings (`"datetime64[ns, UTC]"`, `"timestamp[ns][pyarrow]"`) are instance (identity) requests. The hand-maintained legacy alias map shrinks to just `"datetimetz"`/`"datetime64tz"` (the strings that don't parse via `pandas_dtype`).

As a natural consequence, passing an EA class (e.g. `pd.ArrowDtype`, `pd.DatetimeTZDtype`) now works as a "kind" request matching any instance of that class, with no warning. For `StringDtype` and `CategoricalDtype` the kind reading spans backends: `"string"`/`"str"`/`str` also match Arrow `string`/`large_string`/`string_view` columns (as on main, where the `numpy_dtype` unwrap provided this), and `"category"` also matches Arrow `dictionary` columns. This keeps `DataFrame.plot.scatter(..., c=...)` working with Arrow-backed string columns (the scatter include list relies on `"string"`/`"category"`).

`get_dummies` previously relied on the `ArrowDtype` → `numpy_dtype` unwrap to include Arrow string columns via `include=["object"]`. Its two `select_dtypes` calls are replaced by a local `_is_encodable` predicate fed to `BlockManager._get_data_subset`, preserving existing behavior (GH-56273) without reintroducing the cross-class match. The predicate checks `is_object_dtype` last, since it raises for Arrow `string_view` columns (`ArrowDtype.type` is not implemented for them — a pre-existing gap that also makes the eventual encode raise, on main and here alike).

### Behavior changes

- EA instance specs (e.g. `DatetimeTZDtype("UTC")`, `ArrowDtype(pa.int64())`) now match by exact dtype equality. To match "any tz" / "any Arrow int", pass the class instead.
- `select_dtypes(np.int64)` (the numpy *type*) no longer matches `Int64` EA columns — but `select_dtypes("int64")` (the string *kind*) selects both, plus Arrow int64.
- `select_dtypes("datetime")` / `"datetime64"` now match `DatetimeTZDtype` and Arrow timestamp columns in addition to numpy `datetime64`. Previously matched numpy only.
- As fallout of the previous point, `DataFrame.describe()` with the default `include=None` now includes tz-aware datetime columns, consistent with tz-naive ones (which it has included since 2.0). Whatsnew note added under Timezones.

## Test plan

- [x] New tests for GH-40234, GH-59888 (both halves + symmetric Arrow-int case), parameterized-class warning elimination, class-as-kind semantics for `ArrowDtype` / `DatetimeTZDtype` / `CategoricalDtype`, kind-string matching across backends for int64/float64/datetime, and the Python-`str` route under `future.infer_string`
- [x] New tests: `"string"`/`StringDtype` and `"category"`/`CategoricalDtype` matching Arrow-backed columns (include and exclude), scatter `c=` with an Arrow string column, `describe()` default including tz-aware columns
- [x] Existing `test_select_dtypes.py` suite passes (53 pass, 4 existing skips)
- [x] Broader pass across `frame/`, `dtypes/`, `extension/`, `arrays/`, `reshape/`, `plotting/`, `window/`, `io/json/` (85,341 pass, no regressions)
- [x] `pre-commit`, `mypy`, `pyright` clean

review_comments.body: @jbrockmendel can you update the formatting of the top post to turn all github references in actual links? (I think using `GH-xxxx` instead of `GH#xxxx` will also already solve it)
Will do

This seems like a nice rationalization of the logic in select_dtypes! (and something long overdue given this still seems to be largely based on their mostly being numpy dtypes ..)

One quick additional thought: I see that you labeled `"category"` and `"string"` as a "legacy" aliases to keep back compat. But should those not work in general because they are the string representation of that dtype? It might be that we never really documented the string repr of a dtype explicitly as something you can pass to `select_dtypes`, but it seems something logical to support.

However, if we would treat them in a general way, process those strings using `pandas_dtype`, which then are EDtype instances, and then would use equality, that would not work as expected for parametrized dtypes. One option might be to use the class of the EDtype if the string has no parametrization?
Hi, just a quick question — does this PR resolve both issue #40234 and #59888? Thanks!
Yes
There are parts of this I'm not happy with, going to re-do it manually (in smaller steps).  Closing.

+# select_dtypes: string kind specs that name a (numpy kind char, itemsize)
+# pair shared across numpy, masked, and Arrow backends. Used to match by
+# kind rather than by storage class so that e.g. select_dtypes("int64")
+# selects np.int64, Int64, and ArrowDtype(int64) columns alike.
+_SELECT_DTYPES_KIND_BITNESS: dict[str, tuple[str, int]] = {
+    "int8": ("i", 1),
+    "int16": ("i", 2),
+    "int32": ("i", 4),
+    "int64": ("i", 8),
+    "uint8": ("u", 1),
+    "uint16": ("u", 2),
+    "uint32": ("u", 4),
+    "uint64": ("u", 8),
+    "float32": ("f", 4),
+    "float64": ("f", 8),
+    "bool": ("b", 1),
+}
+
+# select_dtypes: kind-only string specs (no specific bitness).
+_SELECT_DTYPES_KIND_CHAR: dict[str, str] = {
+    "datetime": "M",
+    "datetime64": "M",
+    "timedelta": "m",
+    "timedelta64": "m",
+}
+
+
+def _select_dtypes_arrow_kind_match(dtype: ArrowDtype, req: type) -> bool:
+    # An EA class request names a kind, and Arrow string/dictionary columns
+    # belong to the string/categorical kinds, so e.g. select_dtypes("string")
+    # selects ArrowDtype(pa.string()) columns.
+    import pyarrow as pa
+
+    pa_type = dtype.pyarrow_dtype
+    if req is StringDtype:
+        return (
+            pa.types.is_string(pa_type)
+            or pa.types.is_large_string(pa_type)
+            or pa.types.is_string_view(pa_type)
+        )
+    if req is CategoricalDtype:
+        return pa.types.is_dictionary(pa_type)
+    return False
+
+

def test_select_dtypes_include_exclude_overlap_raises():
    df = DataFrame({"a": [1, 2], "b": pd.array([1, 2], dtype="Int64")})

    # numpy-set overlap
    with pytest.raises(ValueError, match="include and exclude overlap"):
        df.select_dtypes(include=[np.int64], exclude=[np.int64])

    # EA-class overlap
    with pytest.raises(ValueError, match="include and exclude overlap"):
        df.select_dtypes(include=pd.Int64Dtype, exclude=pd.Int64Dtype)

    # EA-instance overlap
    with pytest.raises(ValueError, match="include and exclude overlap"):
        df.select_dtypes(include=pd.Int64Dtype(), exclude=pd.Int64Dtype())

    # kind-string overlap
    with pytest.raises(ValueError, match="include and exclude overlap"):
        df.select_dtypes(include="int64", exclude="int64")


def test_select_dtypes_string_class_matches_arrow_strings():
    # GH#59888: the StringDtype class names the string kind and also matches
    # Arrow-backed string columns
    pa = pytest.importorskip("pyarrow")
    data = {
        # NaN-variant StringDtype (the default "str" dtype under the future
        # string option); construct explicitly so the test does not depend on
        # PANDAS_FUTURE_INFER_STRING (with which off, dtype="str" is object)
        "default": pd.array(["x", "y"], dtype=pd.StringDtype(na_value=np.nan)),
        "nullable": pd.array(["x", "y"], dtype="string"),
        "arrow": pd.array(["x", "y"], dtype=pd.ArrowDtype(pa.string())),
        "large": pd.array(["x", "y"], dtype=pd.ArrowDtype(pa.large_string())),
        "num": [1, 2],
    }
    string_cols = ["default", "nullable", "arrow", "large"]
    if not pa_version_under16p0:
        # pa.string_view requires pyarrow>=16
        data["view"] = pd.array(["x", "y"], dtype=pd.ArrowDtype(pa.string_view()))
        string_cols.append("view")
    df = DataFrame(data)
    # compare columns, not frames: assert_frame_equal raises for string_view
    # columns (ArrowDtype.type is not implemented for string_view)
    result = df.select_dtypes(include=pd.StringDtype)
    assert list(result.columns) == string_cols
    result = df.select_dtypes(exclude=pd.StringDtype)
    assert list(result.columns) == ["num"]

+            "arrow": pd.array([1.0, 2.0], dtype=pd.ArrowDtype(pa.float64())),
+            "narrow": pd.array([1.0, 2.0], dtype="float32"),
+        }
+    )
+    result = df.select_dtypes("float64")
+    tm.assert_frame_equal(result, df[["np", "ea", "arrow"]])
+
+
+def test_select_dtypes_datetime_string_matches_arrow_timestamp():
+    # GH#59888: "datetime" is a kind, matches numpy datetime, DatetimeTZDtype,
+    # and Arrow timestamp alike
+    pa = pytest.importorskip("pyarrow")
+    df = DataFrame(
+        {
+            "naive": pd.to_datetime(["2020-01-01", "2020-01-02"]),
+            "tz": pd.to_datetime(["2020-01-01", "2020-01-02"]).tz_localize("UTC"),
+            "arrow": pd.array(
+                pd.to_datetime(["2020-01-01", "2020-01-02"]),
+                dtype=pd.ArrowDtype(pa.timestamp("ns")),
+            ),
+            "other": [1, 2],
+        }
+    )
+    expected = df[["naive", "tz", "arrow"]]
+    tm.assert_frame_equal(df.select_dtypes(include=["datetime"]), expected)
+    tm.assert_frame_equal(df.select_dtypes(include=["datetime64"]), expected)
+
+
+def test_select_dtypes_arrow_timestamp_string_roundtrip():
+    # GH#59888
+    pytest.importorskip("pyarrow")
+    df = DataFrame(
+        {
+            "id": [1, 2],
+            "ts": pd.to_datetime(["2023-01-01", "2023-01-02"]),
+        }
+    ).astype({"ts": "timestamp[ns][pyarrow]"})
+
+    # The Arrow-string spec should match the Arrow column
+    result = df.select_dtypes(include=["timestamp[ns][pyarrow]"])
+    tm.assert_frame_equal(result, df[["ts"]])
+
+    # A numpy parametrized datetime spec is storage-specific and should not
+    # cross-match an Arrow column
+    result = df.select_dtypes(include=["datetime64[ns]"])
+    assert result.shape[1] == 0
+
+
+def test_select_dtypes_arrow_int_instance_matches_exact():
+    # GH#59888: an Arrow dtype instance matches that exact dtype
+    pa = pytest.importorskip("pyarrow")
+    df = DataFrame(
+        {
+            "np": [1, 2],
+            "arrow": pd.array([1, 2], dtype=pd.ArrowDtype(pa.int64())),
+        }
+    )
+    result = df.select_dtypes(include=[pd.ArrowDtype(pa.int64())])
+    tm.assert_frame_equal(result, df[["arrow"]])
+
+
+def test_select_dtypes_numpy_int_type_does_not_match_arrow():
+    # GH#59888 (symmetric case): the numpy *type* targets numpy storage
+    pa = pytest.importorskip("pyarrow")
+    df = DataFrame(
+        {
+            "np": [1, 2],
+            "arrow": pd.array([1, 2], dtype=pd.ArrowDtype(pa.int64())),
+        }
+    )
+    result = df.select_dtypes(include=[np.int64])
+    tm.assert_frame_equal(result, df[["np"]])
+
+
+def test_select_dtypes_arrow_dtype_class_matches_all_arrow():
+    # Passing the ArrowDtype class as a "kind" request matches every
+    # Arrow-backed column regardless of the inner pyarrow type.
+    pa = pytest.importorskip("pyarrow")
+    df = DataFrame(
+        {
+            "a": pd.array([1, 2], dtype=pd.ArrowDtype(pa.int64())),
+            "b": pd.array(["x", "y"], dtype=pd.ArrowDtype(pa.string())),
+            "c": [1.0, 2.0],
+        }
+    )
+    result = df.select_dtypes(include=pd.ArrowDtype)
+    tm.assert_frame_equal(result, df[["a", "b"]])
+
+
+def test_select_dtypes_parameterized_ea_class_does_not_warn():
+    # Passing a parameterized EA class (ArrowDtype, DatetimeTZDtype, ...)
+    # as a spec previously emitted a UserWarning from pandas_dtype.
+    pa = pytest.importorskip("pyarrow")
+    df = DataFrame({"x": pd.array([1, 2], dtype=pd.ArrowDtype(pa.int64()))})
+    with tm.assert_produces_warning(None):
+        df.select_dtypes(pd.ArrowDtype)
+
+    df2 = DataFrame({"t": pd.to_datetime(["2020-01-01"]).tz_localize("UTC")})
+    with tm.assert_produces_warning(None):
+        df2.select_dtypes(pd.DatetimeTZDtype)
+
+
+def test_select_dtypes_datetimetz_class_matches_any_tz():
+    df = DataFrame(
+        {
+            "utc": pd.to_datetime(["2020-01-01"]).tz_localize("UTC"),
+            "est": pd.to_datetime(["2020-01-01"]).tz_localize("US/Eastern"),
+            "naive": pd.to_datetime(["2020-01-01"]),
+        }
+    )
+    expected = df[["utc", "est"]]
+    tm.assert_frame_equal(df.select_dtypes(pd.DatetimeTZDtype), expected)
+    tm.assert_frame_equal(df.select_dtypes("datetimetz"), expected)
+    tm.assert_frame_equal(df.select_dtypes("datetime64tz"), expected)
+
+
+def test_select_dtypes_category_class_matches_any_categories():
+    df = DataFrame(
+        {
+            "cat1": pd.Categorical(["a", "b"], categories=["a", "b", "c"]),
+            "cat2": pd.Categorical([1, 2], categories=[1, 2, 3]),
+            "num": [1, 2],
+        }
+    )
+    expected = df[["cat1", "cat2"]]
+    tm.assert_frame_equal(df.select_dtypes(pd.CategoricalDtype), expected)
+    tm.assert_frame_equal(df.select_dtypes("category"), expected)
+
+
+def test_select_dtypes_python_type_resolves_to_ea(using_infer_string):
+    # The Python `str` type resolves to StringDtype under future.infer_string
+    # and should select string columns without raising.
+    if not using_infer_string:
+        pytest.skip("requires future.infer_string")
+    df = DataFrame({"s": ["a", "b"], "n": [1, 2]})
+    result = df.select_dtypes(include=str)
+    tm.assert_frame_equal(result, df[["s"]])
+
+
+def test_select_dtypes_include_exclude_overlap_raises():
+    df = DataFrame({"a": [1, 2], "b": pd.array([1, 2], dtype="Int64")})
+
+    # numpy-set overlap
+    with pytest.raises(ValueError, match="include and exclude overlap"):
+        df.select_dtypes(include=[np.int64], exclude=[np.int64])
+
+    # EA-class overlap
+    with pytest.raises(ValueError, match="include and exclude overlap"):
+        df.select_dtypes(include=pd.Int64Dtype, exclude=pd.Int64Dtype)
+
+    # EA-instance overlap
+    with pytest.raises(ValueError, match="include and exclude overlap"):
+        df.select_dtypes(include=pd.Int64Dtype(), exclude=pd.Int64Dtype())
+
+    # kind-string overlap
+    with pytest.raises(ValueError, match="include and exclude overlap"):
+        df.select_dtypes(include="int64", exclude="int64")
+
+
+def test_select_dtypes_string_class_matches_arrow_strings():
+    # GH#59888: the StringDtype class names the string kind and also matches
+    # Arrow-backed string columns
+    pa = pytest.importorskip("pyarrow")
+    data = {
+        # NaN-variant StringDtype (the default "str" dtype under the future
+        # string option); construct explicitly so the test does not depend on
+        # PANDAS_FUTURE_INFER_STRING (with which off, dtype="str" is object)
+        "default": pd.array(["x", "y"], dtype=pd.StringDtype(na_value=np.nan)),
+        "nullable": pd.array(["x", "y"], dtype="string"),
+        "arrow": pd.array(["x", "y"], dtype=pd.ArrowDtype(pa.string())),
+        "large": pd.array(["x", "y"], dtype=pd.ArrowDtype(pa.large_string())),
+        "num": [1, 2],
+    }
+    string_cols = ["default", "nullable", "arrow", "large"]
+    if not pa_version_under16p0:
+        # pa.string_view requires pyarrow>=16
+        data["view"] = pd.array(["x", "y"], dtype=pd.ArrowDtype(pa.string_view()))
+        string_cols.append("view")
+    df = DataFrame(data)
+    # compare columns, not frames: assert_frame_equal raises for string_view
+    # columns (ArrowDtype.type is not implemented for string_view)
+    result = df.select_dtypes(include=pd.StringDtype)
+    assert list(result.columns) == string_cols
+    result = df.select_dtypes(exclude=pd.StringDtype)
+    assert list(result.columns) == ["num"]
+
+
+def test_select_dtypes_string_kind_matches_arrow_strings():
+    # GH#59888: the "string" kind name also matches Arrow-backed string columns
+    pa = pytest.importorskip("pyarrow")
+    data = {
+        # NaN-variant StringDtype (the default "str" dtype under the future
+        # string option); construct explicitly so the test does not depend on
+        # PANDAS_FUTURE_INFER_STRING (with which off, dtype="str" is object)
+        "default": pd.array(["x", "y"], dtype=pd.StringDtype(na_value=np.nan)),
+        "nullable": pd.array(["x", "y"], dtype="string"),
+        "arrow": pd.array(["x", "y"], dtype=pd.ArrowDtype(pa.string())),
+        "large": pd.array(["x", "y"], dtype=pd.ArrowDtype(pa.large_string())),
+        "num": [1, 2],
+    }
+    string_cols = ["default", "nullable", "arrow", "large"]
+    if not pa_version_under16p0:
+        # pa.string_view requires pyarrow>=16
+        data["view"] = pd.array(["x", "y"], dtype=pd.ArrowDtype(pa.string_view()))
+        string_cols.append("view")
+    df = DataFrame(data)
+    # compare columns, not frames: assert_frame_equal raises for string_view
+    # columns (ArrowDtype.type is not implemented for string_view)
+    result = df.select_dtypes(include="string")
+    assert list(result.columns) == string_cols
+    result = df.select_dtypes(exclude="string")
+    assert list(result.columns) == ["num"]
+
+
+def test_select_dtypes_category_class_matches_arrow_dictionary():
+    # GH#59888: the CategoricalDtype class also matches Arrow dictionary columns
+    pa = pytest.importorskip("pyarrow")
+    df = DataFrame(
+        {
+            "cat": pd.Categorical(["a", "b"]),
+            "arrow": pd.array(
+                ["a", "b"],
+                dtype=pd.ArrowDtype(pa.dictionary(pa.int32(), pa.string())),
+            ),
+            "num": [1, 2],
+        }
+    )
+    expected = df[["cat", "arrow"]]
+    tm.assert_frame_equal(df.select_dtypes(include=pd.CategoricalDtype), expected)

review_comments.body: @jbrockmendel can you update the formatting of the top post to turn all github references in actual links? (I think using `GH-xxxx` instead of `GH#xxxx` will also already solve it)
Will do

This seems like a nice rationalization of the logic in select_dtypes! (and something long overdue given this still seems to be largely based on their mostly being numpy dtypes ..)

One quick additional thought: I see that you labeled `"category"` and `"string"` as a "legacy" aliases to keep back compat. But should those not work in general because they are the string representation of that dtype? It might be that we never really documented the string repr of a dtype explicitly as something you can pass to `select_dtypes`, but it seems something logical to support.

However, if we would treat them in a general way, process those strings using `pandas_dtype`, which then are EDtype instances, and then would use equality, that would not work as expected for parametrized dtypes. One option might be to use the class of the EDtype if the string has no parametrization?
Hi, just a quick question — does this PR resolve both issue #40234 and #59888? Thanks!
Yes
